sourceNotes: Bihar 2006 scenario split news items (2006-01 to 2010-12)

newsItems.title: New State Cabinet Orders Audits of Prior Welfare Schemes
newsItems.description: The newly sworn-in JD(U)-BJP state cabinet orders direct audits of all welfare schemes, cooperative loans, and road projects sanctioned under prior regimes.

reactionOptions.text: Launch vigilance department probes against suspected contractors and freeze pending payments.

risk.badOutcome: Contractor associations strike, halting minor maintenance works across cities.

reactionOptions.text: Label the audits as a political vendetta aimed at slandering RJD leaders before local polls.

risk.badOutcome: Audit teams expose registry anomalies in two blocks, weakening the defense.

reactionOptions.text: Propose hiring an independent national agency to conduct state audits to ensure transparency.

risk.badOutcome: Auditing board setup delays keep the investigative reports pending.

reactionOptions.text: No comment.

newsItems.title: Government Announces 50% Women's Reservation in Panchayats
newsItems.description: In a historic move, Chief Minister Nitish Kumar announces a cabinet decision to reserve 50% of all Panchayati Raj seats for women candidates in Bihar.

reactionOptions.text: Enforce the reservation rules and organize district-wide training camps for women sarpanch candidates.

risk.badOutcome: Opposition claims male relatives will continue to act as proxy decision makers.

reactionOptions.text: Demand dedicated sub-quotas for EBC and Dalit women within the 50% reservation pool.

risk.badOutcome: Ruling party points to existing caste reservations, reducing impact of criticism.

reactionOptions.text: Propose an all-party charter to dedicate 30% of village development funds exclusively to women's welfare.

risk.badOutcome: Village council disputes prevent the consensus charter from being implemented.

reactionOptions.text: No comment.

newsItems.title: Panchayat Election Campaigns Focus on Women Candidates
newsItems.description: With local body polls scheduled, campaigns heat up in rural wards. Political parties focus on mobilizing rural women candidates for reserved seats.

reactionOptions.text: Campaign on the government's historic reservation, calling it a social silent revolution.

risk.badOutcome: Opposition highlights poor conditions of rural primary schools and roads.

reactionOptions.text: Highlight that despite reservations, rural panchayats lack basic office spaces and funds.

risk.badOutcome: Voters welcome the women reservation benefits, reducing opposition impact.

reactionOptions.text: Propose forming cross-party district committees to monitor polling booths on election day.

risk.badOutcome: Clashes between student wings outside booths lead to localized delays.

reactionOptions.text: No comment.

newsItems.title: Government Launches Balika Cycle Yojana For Schoolgirls
newsItems.description: The Bihar government launches the Mukhyamantri Balika Cycle Yojana, providing a cash grant of Rs 2,000 directly to schoolgirls to purchase bicycles.

reactionOptions.text: Rollout the grants directly through bank drafts at school events and monitor registrations.

risk.badOutcome: Reports of local bicycle retailers inflating prices draw newspaper criticism.

reactionOptions.text: Demand the state distribute actual bicycles instead of cash, alleging that cash is spent on other needs.

risk.badOutcome: Logistical costs of storage and transit delay cycle delivery by six months.

reactionOptions.text: Propose constructing dedicated bicycle stands and deploying road safety guards along rural school routes.

risk.badOutcome: Lack of police staff prevents regular route security patrols in remote blocks.

reactionOptions.text: No comment.

newsItems.title: Record Number of Women Candidates Win Panchayat Elections
newsItems.description: Panchayat poll results are declared, showing that women candidates have won over 50% of the seats, altering rural leadership profiles.

reactionOptions.text: Declare this as the victory of sushasan, and announce immediate capacity building workshops.

risk.badOutcome: Reports of proxy rule by husbands trigger satirical press reports.

reactionOptions.text: Demand separate panchayat office buildings to allow women sarpanchs to work without family interference.

risk.badOutcome: Finance department states funds are constrained, postponing the plan.

reactionOptions.text: Propose forming a district-level advisory council of women leaders to recommend rural health priorities.

risk.badOutcome: Meetings face low attendance due to lack of local transport facilities.

reactionOptions.text: No comment.

newsItems.title: Government Appoints Bandyopadhyay Commission on Land Reforms
newsItems.description: The state cabinet appoints the Bandyopadhyay Commission to study land ceiling laws, sharecropper (bataidars) rights, and surplus land distribution.

reactionOptions.text: Instruct the commission to collect detailed village bataidar logs to secure sharecropper titles.

risk.badOutcome: Landowner groups voice concern, threatening counter protest rallies.

reactionOptions.text: Oppose any radical land laws, arguing they will destroy agrarian peace and polarize villages.

risk.badOutcome: Dalit and landless voter organizations boycott the party's rallies.

reactionOptions.text: Propose promoting voluntary written crop sharing agreements with tax incentives for landowners.

risk.badOutcome: Low trust between landowners and bataidars keeps agreements low.

reactionOptions.text: No comment.

newsItems.title: Government Announces Hiring of 1 Lakh Contract Teachers
newsItems.description: To address primary school teacher deficits, the government announces recruitment of 1 lakh teachers on contract via local panchayat boards.

reactionOptions.text: Fast-track selection through panchayats, prioritizing local district applicants.

risk.badOutcome: Allegations of bribery in local panchayat selections trigger police inquiries.

reactionOptions.text: Demand recruitment through state service commission (BPSC), calling contract hiring substandard.

risk.badOutcome: Unemployed youth welcome the quick contract jobs, ignoring BPSC debates.

reactionOptions.text: Propose introducing a mandatory Teacher Eligibility Test (TET) to screen selection quality.

risk.badOutcome: Delay in testing schedules postpones the active recruitment rollout.

reactionOptions.text: No comment.

newsItems.title: State Cabinet Clears Bela Industrial Area Revival Plan
newsItems.description: The cabinet approves fiscal incentives and power supply guarantees to revive closed food-processing and leather units in Muzaffarpur's Bela zone.

reactionOptions.text: Allocate state grants and direct banks to offer low-interest working capital loans to units.

risk.badOutcome: Delay in industrial grid connection limits the actual factory startups.

reactionOptions.text: Demand that state funds be spent on rural handloom weavers instead of urban private factory owners.

risk.badOutcome: Trade chambers criticize the opposition as anti-industrialization.

reactionOptions.text: Propose setting up a joint training institute inside Bela to train local youth for factory jobs.

risk.badOutcome: Construction delays keep the training center shell empty for months.

reactionOptions.text: No comment.

newsItems.title: Special Fast-Track Courts Deliver First Criminal Convictions
newsItems.description: The state-appointed fast-track courts deliver speed convictions of several high-profile gang leaders, boosting the government's law record.

reactionOptions.text: Publicize the trial speed on media, asserting the end of political criminal patronage in Bihar.

risk.badOutcome: Opposition claims selective targetting of specific community leaders.

reactionOptions.text: Demand that the fast-track courts prioritize cases against ruling coalition MLAs as well.

risk.badOutcome: Voters welcome the convictions of known gang lords, reducing impact.

reactionOptions.text: Propose a centralized digitized warrant tracking database to prevent police delay in court summons.

risk.badOutcome: Database errors and slow server links keep block stations offline.

reactionOptions.text: No comment.

newsItems.title: Free Generic Medicine Distribution Launched at Block PHCs
newsItems.description: The health department launches a scheme to supply 50 essential generic medicines for free to all outpatients at Primary Health Centers.

reactionOptions.text: Establish state supply depots and set up local drug quality testing boards.

risk.badOutcome: Audit reports expose supply of expired medicines in two blocks, drawing bad press.

reactionOptions.text: Demand hiring permanent doctors first, calling free medicine useless without diagnostic checks.

risk.badOutcome: PHC patients welcome the free medicines, ignoring doctor vacancy debates.

reactionOptions.text: Propose running mobile medical vans to distribute medicines directly to remote hamlets.

risk.badOutcome: Vans break down due to poor road conditions in interior blocks.

reactionOptions.text: No comment.

newsItems.title: Gandhi Setu Repairs Lead to Heavy Traffic Diversions
newsItems.description: Major structural repairs on the Mahatma Gandhi Setu bridge trigger gridlocks. Commuters face hours of delay crossing the Ganges.

reactionOptions.text: Deploy extra traffic police, and order pontoon bridges to be set up for light vehicles.

risk.badOutcome: Pontoon bridge installations are delayed by high river currents.

reactionOptions.text: Demand the Centre sanction a new parallel bridge immediately, calling repairs temporary eyewash.

risk.badOutcome: Central ministry states feasibility studies must be completed first.

reactionOptions.text: Propose restricting heavy freight trucks to night hours only, keeping daytime clear for passenger cars.

risk.badOutcome: Truck driver unions strike, blockading highway toll plazas.

reactionOptions.text: No comment.

newsItems.title: RJD Rallies in Patna Alleging Selective Police Harassment
newsItems.description: RJD cadres stage dharnas in Patna, accusing the NDA state government of using local police to selectively target its leaders and workers.

reactionOptions.text: Refuse the allegations, stating that police actions are based on court warrants and crime records.

risk.badOutcome: Vigilance raids on opposition leaders draw critical coverage from sympathetic media.

reactionOptions.text: Condemn the regime, calling it an attempt to suppress subaltern political voice.

risk.badOutcome: NDA releases lists of criminal records of the arrested cadres, neutralizing charges.

reactionOptions.text: Propose setting up a bipartisan police complaints board to screen political harassment charges.

risk.badOutcome: Home department opposes BPSC guidelines inclusion, stalling the board.

reactionOptions.text: No comment.

newsItems.title: Assembly Passes Landmark Nalanda University Revival Bill
newsItems.description: The Bihar Assembly passes a bill to revive the historical Nalanda University as a global center of excellence, attracting international interest.

reactionOptions.text: Allocate land in Rajgir and form an international mentor group led by Nobel laureates.

risk.badOutcome: Local land acquisitions in Rajgir spark minor protests from potato farmers.

reactionOptions.text: Demand that state funds be spent on regular state universities facing acute teacher vacancies.

risk.badOutcome: Literary bodies criticize the party for opposing a prestige educational project.

reactionOptions.text: Propose that the university reserve 30% of its research fellowships exclusively for state students.

risk.badOutcome: Mentor group rejects state-wise quotas to protect global ranking status.

reactionOptions.text: No comment.

newsItems.title: Bandyopadhyay Commission Leak Sparks Landlord Alarms
newsItems.description: Leaked sections of the Bandyopadhyay land reform commission report suggest lowering land ceiling limits, triggering panic among large landholders.

reactionOptions.text: Clarify that the report is recommendatory only and no sudden land laws will be passed.

risk.badOutcome: Sharecropper organizations accuse the government of yielding to landowner lobbies.

reactionOptions.text: Demand the government formally reject the ceiling cuts to protect agricultural investment stability.

risk.badOutcome: Dalit voter bases shift away, accusing the party of protecting feudal land ownership.

reactionOptions.text: Propose setting up an all-party committee with farmer and landowner reps to study the clauses.

risk.badOutcome: Tensions between groups disrupt the dialogue sessions, halting progress.

reactionOptions.text: No comment.

newsItems.title: Opposition Accuses Nitish Kumar of Favoring Forward Castes
newsItems.description: Opposition parties hold rallies in Patna, alleging that the JD(U)-BJP administration is systematically favoring forward castes in government postings.

reactionOptions.text: Advertise data on recent EBC and Dalit postings, defending the social justice balance of the cabinet.

risk.badOutcome: Counter protests by elite associations polarize district voter pools.

reactionOptions.text: Demand a caste-wise audit of all secretarial appointments to expose the representation deficit.

risk.badOutcome: Governing allies release lists of previous RJD postings, neutralizing charges.

reactionOptions.text: Propose formulating a dedicated Mahadalit category to target welfare directly to the poorest.

risk.badOutcome: Other backward classes protest the new category, fearing dilution of their share.

reactionOptions.text: No comment.

newsItems.title: Government Formulates Plan to Appoint Mahadalit Commission
newsItems.description: To address the poorest sections of Scheduled Castes, the state government announces plans to set up a Mahadalit Commission to recommend targeted welfare.

reactionOptions.text: Appoint the commissioners and allocate initial budget grants to study Dalit block populations.

risk.badOutcome: Paswan groups protest their exclusion from initial Mahadalit category drafts.

reactionOptions.text: Label the commission as a political attempt to divide the Dalit vote share for electoral gain.

risk.badOutcome: Non-Paswan Dalit groups criticize the party for opposing targeted welfare.

reactionOptions.text: Propose that the budget grants be increased for all Scheduled Castes equally, bypassing new categories.

risk.badOutcome: Debates over community quotas delay the release of general SC funds.

reactionOptions.text: No comment.

newsItems.title: State Reopens 1989 Bhagalpur Riots Cases; Key Accused Booked
newsItems.description: The state home department reopens several closed cases of the 1989 Bhagalpur communal riots, initiating fast-track trials against key accused.

reactionOptions.text: Enforce prosecutions strictly and announce welfare pensions for riot-affected survivors.

risk.badOutcome: Conservative factions within coalition partner BJP voice concerns over timing.

reactionOptions.text: Demand prosecution of the administrative officers who colluded in closing cases earlier.

risk.badOutcome: IPS unions protest, warning that prosecuting retired officers defames the cadre.

reactionOptions.text: Propose establishing a statutory Communal Harmony Board to monitor peace in Bhagalpur.

risk.badOutcome: Rival religious groups boycott board meetings, keeping local trust low.

reactionOptions.text: No comment.

newsItems.title: Launch of Mukhyamantri Gram Sampark Yojana for Rural Roads
newsItems.description: The rural development department launches a major program to construct all-weather concrete roads linking isolated hamlets to nearby highways.

reactionOptions.text: Allocate state funds, and mandate local block panchayat boards to inspect road quality.

risk.badOutcome: Panchayat selection of road routes triggers local caste disputes in blocks.

reactionOptions.text: Demand that state engineers audit road materials, alleging usage of substandard local clay.

risk.badOutcome: Contractors suspend work, protesting against arbitrary inspection checks.

reactionOptions.text: Propose employing local landless laborers for road construction to boost rural wages.

risk.badOutcome: Lack of technical expertise among local laborers results in poor road life.

reactionOptions.text: No comment.

newsItems.title: Gandak Canal Breach Floods Agricultural Darbhanga
newsItems.description: Heavy seasonal rainfall breaches the Gandak canal system, submerging standing crops and flooding rural villages in Darbhanga district.

reactionOptions.text: Deploy emergency canal repair teams, distribute dry food packets, and waive local crop cess.

risk.badOutcome: Bureaucratic delay in checking crop losses sparks farmer sit-ins in town.

reactionOptions.text: Demand that the state immediately release Rs 3,000 per acre compensation for ruined crops.

risk.badOutcome: Finance department states canal funds are already committed, delaying relief.

reactionOptions.text: Propose a long-term concrete lining project for all major Darbhanga canal banks.

risk.badOutcome: Construction shutdown of canals during lining triggers farmer water protests.

reactionOptions.text: No comment.

newsItems.title: State Demands Rs 2,000 Crore Flood Package From Centre
newsItems.description: Following damages in Darbhanga and Champaran, the Bihar Cabinet passes a resolution demanding a Rs 2,000 crore relief package from UPA.

reactionOptions.text: Hold conventions to demand central allocation, warning against step-motherly treatment.

risk.badOutcome: Opposition accuses the state of failing to submit previous audit reports.

reactionOptions.text: Demand a central CAG audit of state relief spending, alleging diversion of funds.

risk.badOutcome: Rural voters view the opposition as trying to block central package release.

reactionOptions.text: Propose sending a joint treasury-opposition delegation to Delhi to lobby for the package.

risk.badOutcome: Leader disagreements over draft memorandum prevent the delegation from flying.

reactionOptions.text: No comment.

newsItems.title: Balika Cycle Yojana Expanded to Minority and Backward Classes
newsItems.description: Following the success of the schoolgirl cycle scheme, the government announces its expansion to girl students of minority and EBC sections.

reactionOptions.text: Rollout the expanded grants directly through bank drafts at school events.

risk.badOutcome: General category student unions protest, demanding merit-based cycles.

reactionOptions.text: Demand that the cycle scheme be made income-based, excluding affluent families within backwards.

risk.badOutcome: Minority voter organizations accuse the party of trying to dilute welfare benefits.

reactionOptions.text: Propose that the state buy school buses for rural girls instead of distributing cycles.

risk.badOutcome: High fuel and maintenance costs keep the buses off rural routes.

reactionOptions.text: No comment.

newsItems.title: Government Starts Massive Police Recruitment Drive
newsItems.description: The home department announces a massive recruitment drive to fill 15,000 vacant constable posts, introducing biometric security checks.

reactionOptions.text: Enforce transparent recruitment rules, deploy CCTVs, and publicize results on state portals.

risk.badOutcome: Failed applicants stage protest rallies, alleging evaluation anomalies.

reactionOptions.text: Demand judicial monitoring of recruitment, alleging that ruling allies are selling slots.

risk.badOutcome: Voters welcome the transparent biometric selection, reducing impact.

reactionOptions.text: Propose allocating 20% of recruitment budget to build modern physical training yards in Gaya.

risk.badOutcome: Construction delays keep the new training yards incomplete for months.

reactionOptions.text: No comment.

newsItems.title: Contract Teachers Strike in Patna Demanding Equal Pay
newsItems.description: Newly recruited primary contract teachers launch protest strikes in Patna, demanding equal pay scales matching regular state teachers.

reactionOptions.text: Refuse the pay hike, citing state budget constraints, and warn of contract terminations.

risk.badOutcome: Unions block primary school doors, shutting down classes in three districts.

reactionOptions.text: Support the teachers, demanding the government form an assembly committee to negotiate pay.

risk.badOutcome: Public feels unsympathetic to strike, citing closed classes for their kids.

reactionOptions.text: Propose a performance-linked pay scale where contract teachers can secure regular scales after tests.

risk.badOutcome: Teacher unions reject testing clauses, continuing the strike.

reactionOptions.text: No comment.

newsItems.title: Tourism Department Launches Patna Zoo Modernization Plan
newsItems.description: The tourism department approves a modernization plan for the Sanjay Gandhi Biological Park, constructing new enclosures and safari tracks.

reactionOptions.text: Allocate state funds, and introduce battery-operated visitor vehicles inside the park.

risk.badOutcome: Animal activists protest the noise of construction works inside animal zones.

reactionOptions.text: Demand that state funds be spent on district parks first instead of centralizing budgets in Patna.

risk.badOutcome: Patna citizens welcome the zoo upgrade, criticizing the opposition.

reactionOptions.text: Propose a model allowing private corporates to adopt and maintain animal enclosures.

risk.badOutcome: Lack of corporate head offices in Patna results in low adoption rates.

reactionOptions.text: No comment.

newsItems.title: Government Hosts Global Bihar Summit to Attract Investment
newsItems.description: The state government hosts the first Global Bihar Summit in Patna, inviting NRI entrepreneurs and national business leaders to invest.

reactionOptions.text: Announce single-window clearance rules and tax waivers for agro-based business units.

risk.badOutcome: Opposition highlights poor electricity supply index, keeping investors skeptical.

reactionOptions.text: Expose land acquisitions, alleging that fertile farming lands are being sold to corporates.

risk.badOutcome: Trade bodies criticize the opposition, stating it blocks industrial growth.

reactionOptions.text: Propose establishing a State Skill Development Mission to train rural youth for incoming jobs.

risk.badOutcome: Administrative delays in hiring trainers keep training centers idle.

reactionOptions.text: No comment.

newsItems.title: Mahadalit Commission Identifies 18 Castes For Special Welfare
newsItems.description: The Mahadalit Commission submits its report, identifying 18 scheduled castes as 'Mahadalit' and recommending exclusive welfare programs.

reactionOptions.text: Accept the report and announce special grants for house construction and scholarships.

risk.badOutcome: Paswan community organizations hold protests, demanding their inclusion in lists.

reactionOptions.text: Label the report as a political tool to split the Dalit vote bank and isolate specific leaders.

risk.badOutcome: Non-Paswan Dalit groups criticize the party for trying to block benefits.

reactionOptions.text: Propose increasing general Dalit welfare budgets by 30% instead of dividing the category.

risk.badOutcome: Debates over community shares stall the release of current SC funds.

reactionOptions.text: No comment.

newsItems.title: Government Announces Free Radios for Mahadalit Families
newsItems.description: CM Nitish Kumar announces a scheme to distribute free transistor radios to Mahadalit families to promote state educational broadcasts.

reactionOptions.text: Rollout the radio distribution drives through block welfare officers.

risk.badOutcome: Audit exposes corruption in procurement of substandard radio sets, drawing press.

reactionOptions.text: Demand the government distribute cash allowances instead, calling radios a cosmetic gimmick.

risk.badOutcome: Welfare organizations point to high risk of cash diversion by male heads.

reactionOptions.text: Propose setting up panchayat community radio stations to broadcast local farming tips.

risk.badOutcome: Licensing delays from the central ministry keep the stations silent.

reactionOptions.text: No comment.

newsItems.title: Union Railway Minister Lalu Prasad Launches Chhapra Coach Factory
newsItems.description: Union Railway Minister Lalu Prasad Yadav lays the foundation stone for a mega wheel and coach factory in Chhapra, highlighting UPA's contributions.

reactionOptions.text: Welcome the project, and demand the Centre expedite matching funds for state feeder lines.

risk.badOutcome: Opposition claims state police are delaying land clearances for factory access.

reactionOptions.text: Campaign aggressively on the Chhapra factory, highlighting Lalu Prasad's railway turnaround.

risk.badOutcome: NDA releases data showing slow progress of previous local rail lines.

reactionOptions.text: Propose a joint board representing state planners and railway officials to build bypasses around factory.

risk.badOutcome: Jurisdictional disputes between state and central boards delay bypass design.

reactionOptions.text: No comment.

newsItems.title: Urea Scarcity Triggers Farmer Rallies in South Bihar
newsItems.description: Shortage of fertilizer (urea) during the planting season causes farmer agitations in Gaya and Rohtas. Opposition blames state distribution.

reactionOptions.text: Procure emergency urea stocks from national pools and deploy police guards at distribution centers.

risk.badOutcome: Black marketing by local cooperative heads continues, drawing bad press.

reactionOptions.text: Lead farmer sit-ins outside agriculture department offices, demanding subsidy checks.

risk.badOutcome: Clashes between farmers and security staff lead to police lathi-charge.

reactionOptions.text: Propose issuing digitized slips linked to land records to prevent non-farmers from buying.

risk.badOutcome: Frequent power failures at rural centers keep system terminals offline.

reactionOptions.text: No comment.

newsItems.title: Armed Attack on Rural Police Station in Rohtas
newsItems.description: Suspected Naxal factions attack a rural police outpost in Rohtas district, looting armaments and triggering search operations.

reactionOptions.text: Deploy special task force combing operations, seal borders, and suspend negligent station chiefs.

risk.badOutcome: Local farm workers allege STF search excesses and harassment in villages.

reactionOptions.text: Condemn the police infrastructure, alleging that rural outposts lack basic communication lines.

risk.badOutcome: Security analysts accuse the opposition of trying to politicize a terror incident.

reactionOptions.text: Propose forming village defense committees with licensed arms for village youths.

risk.badOutcome: Local political disputes prevent the consensus selection of committee heads.

reactionOptions.text: No comment.

newsItems.title: Government Introduces Spoken English Classes in Schools
newsItems.description: To improve employability, the education department partners with the British Council to run spoken English classes in senior state schools.

reactionOptions.text: Allocate funds to train state teachers and distribute spoken audio cassettes to schools.

risk.badOutcome: Lack of functional audio players in rural schools limits the scheme.

reactionOptions.text: Demand that the state hire basic science and math teachers first, calling English a cosmetic push.

risk.badOutcome: Urban student unions welcome English classes, criticizing the opposition.

reactionOptions.text: Propose private IT sector employees volunteer to mentor senior students on weekends.

risk.badOutcome: Lack of private IT offices outside Patna limits the mentoring reach.

reactionOptions.text: No comment.

newsItems.title: Kosi River Embankment Breach Deluges North Bihar
newsItems.description: The Kosi river breaches its embankment at Kushaha in Nepal, shifting its course and flooding five North Bihar districts, affecting 30 lakh people.

reactionOptions.text: Order national rescue help, deploy state boats, and set up mega relief camps in highlands.

risk.badOutcome: Shortage of rescue boats delays operations in Saharsa, drawing critical press.

reactionOptions.text: Demand the PM declare the breach a national calamity and release immediate Rs 2,000 crore relief.

risk.badOutcome: Central agencies delay declarations to assess damage, slowing relief flows.

reactionOptions.text: Propose a joint scientific commission of Nepal and Indian engineers to redirect the river.

risk.badOutcome: Diplomatic protocol delays postpone the first meeting of the commission.

reactionOptions.text: No comment.

newsItems.title: Army Deployed in Saharsa For Kosi Flood Rescue
newsItems.description: Army columns and NDRF battalions are deployed to rescue stranded families in Saharsa, Madhepura, and Supaul. Deluge is declared a National Calamity.

reactionOptions.text: Coordinate state relief cell with Army command, and allocate Rs 100 crore for medicine stocks.

risk.badOutcome: Distribution delays in remote blocks spark minor protest rallies by victims.

reactionOptions.text: Expose supply contracts, alleging that ruling party candidates are selling relief grain.

risk.badOutcome: Victims express anger, stating the opposition is exploiting their misery.

reactionOptions.text: Propose forming an all-party legislative committee to inspect relief camp distributions.

risk.badOutcome: Rival party workers clash at camp gates, disrupting relief distributions.

reactionOptions.text: No comment.

newsItems.title: State Launches World Bank Funded Kosi Recovery Project
newsItems.description: The state government signs an agreement for a World Bank funded Kosi Flood Recovery Project to rebuild houses and roads in affected zones.

reactionOptions.text: Allocate reconstruction subsidies directly to beneficiary bank accounts to rebuild concrete homes.

risk.badOutcome: Delays in verification of beneficiary titles stall the initial payouts.

reactionOptions.text: Demand a probe into reconstruction tenders, alleging favoritism of specific contractors.

risk.badOutcome: Contractors strike, halting current road rebuilding works.

reactionOptions.text: Propose that local village committees audit building materials and progress directly.

risk.badOutcome: Panchayat heads decline oversight, citing lack of training in rules.

reactionOptions.text: No comment.

newsItems.title: CM Clarifies Land Ceilings Will Not Be Lowered
newsItems.description: Seeking to address landowner concerns, Chief Minister Nitish Kumar clarifies that the government will not lower existing land ceiling limits.

reactionOptions.text: Declare the decision as necessary to protect farm investments and maintain agrarian peace.

risk.badOutcome: Sharecropper organizations hold black flag rallies, alleging betrayal.

reactionOptions.text: Label the decision as a surrender to feudal lobbies, demanding sharecropper protections.

risk.badOutcome: Land-owning voter bases consolidate support behind the ruling alliance.

reactionOptions.text: Propose a pension scheme for old sharecroppers who surrender cultivation claims.

risk.badOutcome: High financial burden stalls the pension scheme draft in cabinet.

reactionOptions.text: No comment.

newsItems.title: Lalu Prasad Launches Yatras Highlighting Flood Corruption
newsItems.description: RJD Chief Lalu Prasad Yadav launches state-wide yatras in flood-affected districts, alleging corruption in relief distributions.

reactionOptions.text: Counter the yatra by releasing data on completed roads and active relief operations.

risk.badOutcome: Opposition displays photos of unfinished bridges at several yatra stops.

reactionOptions.text: Intensify the yatra rallies, highlighting delayed cash distributions to flood victims.

risk.badOutcome: Clashes between rival cadres at yatra venues draw police actions.

reactionOptions.text: Propose a joint assembly charter to coordinate rebuilding priorities without political battles.

risk.badOutcome: Rivals refuse to sign, continuing political yatra campaigns.

reactionOptions.text: No comment.

newsItems.title: JD(U) and BJP Finalize Lok Sabha Seat Sharing
newsItems.description: Ahead of the general elections, the JD(U) and BJP finalize seat sharing in Bihar, while RJD and LJP form a separate UPA splinter front.

reactionOptions.text: Announce the sharing list and launch joint campaigns focusing on state growth.

risk.badOutcome: Local BJP leaders in two districts rebel over seat transfers to JD(U).

reactionOptions.text: Campaign on the friction between JD(U) and BJP on secular issues, calling them unstable.

risk.badOutcome: NDA resolves the disputes quickly, reducing the impact of criticism.

reactionOptions.text: Propose a campaign code of ethics to restrict personal attacks during rallies.

risk.badOutcome: Rival candidates ignore the code, escalating personal accusations on stage.

reactionOptions.text: No comment.

newsItems.title: Urban Development Cell Proposes Patna Marine Drive
newsItems.description: The urban development cell proposes constructing a major Ganga riverfront expressway (Patna Marine Drive) to link East and West Patna.

reactionOptions.text: Allocate preliminary state funds and initiate surveys for the Ganga riverfront project.

risk.badOutcome: Environmentalists challenge the design, citing threat to Ganga dolphin zones.

reactionOptions.text: Demand that the state guarantee housing rehabilitation plots first to all riverfront slums.

risk.badOutcome: Urban trade chambers criticize the opposition as anti-development.

reactionOptions.text: Propose a public-private partnership model to attract global infrastructure developers.

risk.badOutcome: Private developers demand cheap power guarantees before bidding, delaying project.

reactionOptions.text: No comment.

newsItems.title: Lok Sabha Campaigning Reaches Peak in Bihar
newsItems.description: Campaigning for Bihar Lok Sabha seats reaches peak. NDA projects Nitish Kumar's clean image, while RJD warns of upper-caste domination.

reactionOptions.text: Campaign heavily on developmental achievements, showing road and bridge growth.

risk.badOutcome: Opposition highlights instances of contract teacher strikes in districts.

reactionOptions.text: Highlight social neglect of marginalized Dalits, calling sushasan a media eyewash.

risk.badOutcome: NDA wins several rural seats due to strong EBC support equations.

reactionOptions.text: Propose public TV debates on industrial investments and state revenue indices.

risk.badOutcome: Opponents refuse to attend, leading to empty-chair media campaigns.

reactionOptions.text: No comment.

newsItems.title: Lok Sabha Polling in Gaya and Jamui Under High Security
newsItems.description: Lok Sabha polling in sensitive Naxal belt districts concludes. Heavy security deployments ensure peaceful turnout in rural blocks.

reactionOptions.text: Deploy helicopter surveillance and armed police patrols in sensitive polling blocks.

risk.badOutcome: Local leaders allege police restrictions kept remote voters from polling.

reactionOptions.text: Petition the EC to verify EVM logs, alleging ruling party booth agents siphoned votes.

risk.badOutcome: EC rejects the petition, citing transparent polling agent registries.

reactionOptions.text: Propose that counting centers allow only central paramilitary guards, keeping state police out.

risk.badOutcome: State police unions protest the proposal, calling it an insult to cadre.

reactionOptions.text: No comment.

newsItems.title: NDA Sweeps Bihar Lok Sabha Polls; RJD Reduced to 4 Seats
newsItems.description: The JDU-BJP alliance sweeps the Bihar Lok Sabha polls, winning 32 out of 40 seats. The RJD is reduced to only 4 seats in its worst setback.

reactionOptions.text: Declare the sweep as a triumph of sushasan, and announce new road projects.

risk.badOutcome: Opposition files petitions in court challenging candidate affidavits.

reactionOptions.text: Accept the verdict, stating that RJD will reorganize its cadres to defend secularism.

risk.badOutcome: Local party workers express frustration over losing cabinet influence.

reactionOptions.text: Propose a joint assembly memo to petition the Centre for Special Category Status.

risk.badOutcome: Fierce rivalries between leaders prevent the joint memo draft.

reactionOptions.text: No comment.

newsItems.title: Post-Election Clashes Reported in Rural Vaishali
newsItems.description: Following the Lok Sabha results, clashes between RJD and JD(U) cadres are reported in rural Vaishali. District borders are sealed by police.

reactionOptions.text: Deploy police pickets in sensitive blocks and arrest suspected troublemakers.

risk.badOutcome: Opposition claims local police are targetting only their cadre.

reactionOptions.text: Demand a judicial probe, alleging that local police colluded with ruling candidates during raids.

risk.badOutcome: Ruling party releases videos of opposition cadres instigating the clashes.

reactionOptions.text: Propose organizing joint cross-party peace marches through sensitive blocks.

risk.badOutcome: Extreme factions boycott the marches, reducing their impact on the ground.

reactionOptions.text: No comment.

newsItems.title: Cabinet Initiates Drafting of Public Services Guarantee Act
newsItems.description: The state cabinet approves a plan to draft a Public Services Guarantee Act, penalizing bureaucrats for delays in issuing certificates and utilities.

reactionOptions.text: Fast-track the draft, and set clear cash penalties to be deducted from negligent officer pay.

risk.badOutcome: Bureaucratic lobbies delay filings, protesting against salary deduction rules.

reactionOptions.text: Demand judicial appeal clauses, alleging that block officers will pass bad orders to avoid penalties.

risk.badOutcome: Ruling party points to existing grievance boards, neutralizing criticism.

reactionOptions.text: Propose setting up single-window digitized counters (Vasudha Centers) in rural blocks.

risk.badOutcome: Power cuts and network delays keep Vasudha terminals offline in remote blocks.

reactionOptions.text: No comment.

newsItems.title: Severe Drought Declared in 26 Districts of Bihar
newsItems.description: A failure of monsoon rains triggers severe drought. The state government declares 26 districts drought-affected and plans emergency power supplies.

reactionOptions.text: Increase rural power supply to 8 hours daily for irrigation and waive tubewell cess.

risk.badOutcome: Grid overload causes frequent trippings, failing to maintain the supply hours.

reactionOptions.text: Demand a complete waiver of all farm loans and immediate central aid of Rs 2,000 crore.

risk.badOutcome: Cooperative banks warn of credit freeze if all loans are waived, delaying works.

reactionOptions.text: Propose cash grants for farmers to build farm ponds (Ahars and Pynes) to harvest rain.

risk.badOutcome: Slow excavation work in dry clay soil delays pond benefits.

reactionOptions.text: No comment.

newsItems.title: Government Announces Direct Cash Diesel Subsidy for Farmers
newsItems.description: To save the standing kharif crop during drought, the government announces direct cash diesel subsidies for farmers running pump sets.

reactionOptions.text: Rollout the subsidy through block offices, using land registry records for verification.

risk.badOutcome: Audit exposes corruption in fuel bills, drawing critical local press.

reactionOptions.text: Demand direct bank transfers, alleging block officers siphon cash commissions.

risk.badOutcome: Lack of bank accounts in remote blocks delays the direct transfers.

reactionOptions.text: Propose shifting the budget to solar pumps to reduce agricultural diesel imports.

risk.badOutcome: High solar pump costs keep take-up low despite state subsidies.

reactionOptions.text: No comment.

newsItems.title: Amartya Sen Chairs First Nalanda Mentor Group Meeting
newsItems.description: Nobel Laureate Amartya Sen chairs the first Nalanda University Mentor Group meeting in Patna, finalizing the global curriculum drafts.

reactionOptions.text: Allocate infrastructure development grants and clear Rajgir campus construction contracts.

risk.badOutcome: Landowner litigation in Rajgir delays construction starts.

reactionOptions.text: Demand that the state assembly supervise board expenditures, alleging lack of oversight.

risk.badOutcome: Board states global autonomy rules prohibit direct political control.

reactionOptions.text: Propose setting up an exclusive center within the university to document Bihar's heritage.

risk.badOutcome: Lack of historical document copies delays center research launches.

reactionOptions.text: No comment.

newsItems.title: RJD-LJP Alliance Wins 5 Assembly Bypoll Seats
newsItems.description: In assembly bypolls for 18 seats, the RJD-LJP alliance wins 5 seats, signaling organizational recovery attempts before the 2010 state elections.

reactionOptions.text: Downplay the bypolls, stating that local dynamics do not reflect state development support.

risk.badOutcome: Cadres in polling districts raise concerns over local coordinate gaps.

reactionOptions.text: Campaign on the bypoll victory, calling it the beginning of the end of NDA rule in Bihar.

risk.badOutcome: Governing allies release completed road maps, neutralizing yatra impact.

reactionOptions.text: Propose that the EC double-check counting slips with EVM memory registry logs.

risk.badOutcome: EC rejects the check as unnecessary, citing current protocol safety.

reactionOptions.text: No comment.

newsItems.title: Government Announces Land Allocation Plan for Mahadalits
newsItems.description: The state cabinet approves a scheme to allocate 3 decimals of homestead land to every landless Mahadalit family in Bihar.

reactionOptions.text: Allocate state budgets to purchase private land plots for distribution to families.

risk.badOutcome: Bureaucratic delays in identifying landless families stall actual registry.

reactionOptions.text: Demand that the allocation be increased to 5 decimals, calling 3 decimals too small.

risk.badOutcome: Finance department states extra land purchases exceed budget capacity.

reactionOptions.text: Propose that village panchayats verify beneficiary eligibility to prevent fraud allocations.

risk.badOutcome: Local political blockings prevent regular panchayat audit meetings.

reactionOptions.text: No comment.

newsItems.title: Cycle Scheme Expanded to Backward Class Boy Students
newsItems.description: Following the success of the Balika Cycle Yojana, the government expands the cash cycle grants to boy students of backward and minority classes.

reactionOptions.text: Rollout the grants directly through bank drafts at school events.

risk.badOutcome: General category student unions protest, demanding merit-based cycles.

reactionOptions.text: Demand the state run free student buses instead, calling cycle cash short-term eyewash.

risk.badOutcome: High transport operational costs keep buses off rural routes.

reactionOptions.text: Propose that parent committees monitor cycle purchases and school attendance monthly.

risk.badOutcome: Lack of parents attendance at meetings stalls committee audits.

reactionOptions.text: No comment.

newsItems.title: NDA Launches 'Sushasan' Campaign Highlighting Road Growth
newsItems.description: The JD(U) and BJP launch a state-wide campaign highlighting road construction and electricity index growth achieved in five years.

reactionOptions.text: Organize district development fairs to showcase completed bridges and school upgrades.

risk.badOutcome: Opposition displays photos of unfinished bypasses near fair venues.

reactionOptions.text: Highlight growing state debt and the grievances of contract teachers in rallies.

risk.badOutcome: Public welcomes the visible highway connectivity, reducing impact of rallies.

reactionOptions.text: Propose a special debate on state GDP and finance parameters in the assembly.

risk.badOutcome: Rival slogans disrupt the assembly debate within minutes, stalling the house.

reactionOptions.text: No comment.

newsItems.title: Fast-Track Court Convicts Jehanabad Jailbreak Masterminds
newsItems.description: The fast-track court convicts the masterminds of the historic 2005 Jehanabad prison break case, sentencing them to life terms.

reactionOptions.text: Publicize the convictions on media, asserting the end of gang impunity in Bihar.

risk.badOutcome: Opposition claims selective acceleration of cases against their supporters.

reactionOptions.text: Demand prosecution of the jail officials who colluded in security lapses in 2005.

risk.badOutcome: Jail unions protest, warning that prosecuting guards defames the cadre.

reactionOptions.text: Propose a centralized digitized jail surveillance database to prevent security lapses.

risk.badOutcome: Database errors and slow server links keep block stations offline.

reactionOptions.text: No comment.

newsItems.title: Nitish Kumar Launches State-wide 'Vishwas Yatra'
newsItems.description: CM Nitish Kumar launches the 'Vishwas Yatra' to visit villages directly, inspect developmental works, and hold direct citizen feedback meets.

reactionOptions.text: Hold village chaupals and order immediate action on local block officer complaints.

risk.badOutcome: Opposition groups hold black flag demonstrations at several yatra stops.

reactionOptions.text: Hold rallies along the yatra tracks, highlighting block-level welfare leakages.

risk.badOutcome: Local residents welcome the CM's direct visits, ignoring opposition rallies.

reactionOptions.text: Propose a joint assembly board to monitor village grievance redressals during yatra.

risk.badOutcome: Rival party workers clash at chaupal venues, stalling joint reviews.

reactionOptions.text: No comment.

newsItems.title: Massive Turnout at Gaya Vishwas Yatra Rally
newsItems.description: CM Nitish Kumar's Vishwas Yatra rally in Gaya draws massive crowds of rural voters, showcasing support for the sushasan campaign.

reactionOptions.text: Announce fresh state development grants for Gaya heritage conservation and roads.

risk.badOutcome: Election commission issues warnings regarding announcements close to polls.

reactionOptions.text: Hold counter rallies in Gaya, focusing on dried-up tubewells and farmer debts.

risk.badOutcome: Rival party workers disrupt the venues, leading to localized clashes.

reactionOptions.text: Propose a joint tourism development charter for Gaya, inviting opposition ideas.

risk.badOutcome: Rival boycott of tourism board meetings delays charter implementation.

reactionOptions.text: No comment.

newsItems.title: Nitish Cancels BJP Dinner Over Narendra Modi Posters
newsItems.description: CM Nitish Kumar cancels a scheduled dinner for BJP leaders in Patna, protesting against posters showing him with Narendra Modi, drawing media focus.

reactionOptions.text: Assert the JD(U)'s independent secular identity while maintaining the state alliance.

risk.badOutcome: BJP local units express anger, threatening to review coalition terms.

reactionOptions.text: Label the dispute as opportunistic drama, alleging that JD(U) and BJP are complicit on ground.

risk.badOutcome: NDA leaders hold joint press meets, neutralizing the opportunism charges.

reactionOptions.text: Propose a joint assembly charter to explicitly define secularism rules for state parties.

risk.badOutcome: Legislative disputes delay the charter draft, leaving tensions active.

reactionOptions.text: No comment.

newsItems.title: Mahananda River Floods Rural Seemanchal Districts
newsItems.description: Heavy rainfall in Seemanchal causes the Mahananda river to overflow, flooding agricultural lands and villages in Araria and Purnia districts.

reactionOptions.text: Deploy rescue boats, distribute dry food packets, and waive local crop cess in Seemanchal.

risk.badOutcome: Bureaucratic delay in checking crop losses sparks farmer sit-ins in town.

reactionOptions.text: Demand that the state release Rs 3,000 per acre compensation for ruined crops.

risk.badOutcome: Finance department states flood budgets are already committed, delaying relief.

reactionOptions.text: Propose a joint Nepal-Bihar river safety commission to monitor dam releases during monsoon.

risk.badOutcome: Diplomatic protocols delay the commission meetings, keeping monsoon active.

reactionOptions.text: No comment.

newsItems.title: Assembly Passes Right to Public Services Act
newsItems.description: The Bihar Assembly passes the landmark Right to Public Services Act, guaranteeing delivery of key documents within specified timelines.

reactionOptions.text: Enforce the act, set up monitoring cells in all districts, and penalize delayed officers.

risk.badOutcome: Bureaucratic lobbies delay registries, protesting against penalty rules.

reactionOptions.text: Demand judicial appeal clauses, alleging that block officers will pass bad orders to avoid penalties.

risk.badOutcome: Ruling party points to existing grievance boards, neutralizing criticism.

reactionOptions.text: Propose extending single-window digitized counters to all gram panchayat offices.

risk.badOutcome: Power cuts and network delays keep counters offline in remote blocks.

reactionOptions.text: No comment.

newsItems.title: EC Announces Six-Phase Bihar Assembly Election Schedule
newsItems.description: The EC announces a six-phase Bihar assembly election schedule for October-November, setting the code of conduct active.

reactionOptions.text: Campaign on the government's developmental achievements, showing road and bridge growth.

risk.badOutcome: Opposition highlights instances of contract teacher strikes in districts.

reactionOptions.text: Campaign aggressively on social neglect of marginalized Dalits, calling sushasan a media eyewash.

risk.badOutcome: NDA wins several rural seats due to strong EBC support equations.

reactionOptions.text: Propose requesting the EC to install CCTV cameras in all sensitive rural booths.

risk.badOutcome: Technical errors in remote booths leave cameras offline on polling day.

reactionOptions.text: No comment.

newsItems.title: Nitish and Sushil Modi Launch Joint Campaigns
newsItems.description: Displaying strong alliance unity to counter poster disputes, CM Nitish Kumar and BJP leader Sushil Modi launch joint campaigns in Sonepur.

reactionOptions.text: Advertise the coalition's unity and coordinate joint worker meets in all blocks.

risk.badOutcome: RJD candidates split the backward class voter share in key blocks.

reactionOptions.text: Highlight ideological differences between JDU and BJP on secular issues, calling them unstable.

risk.badOutcome: NDA resolves the disputes quickly, reducing the impact of criticism.

reactionOptions.text: Propose drafting a joint agricultural development charter to protect crop support prices.

risk.badOutcome: Delayed hearings prevent the charter from being signed by both camps.

reactionOptions.text: No comment.

newsItems.title: NDA Wins Historic Landslide in Bihar; Nitish Sworn In
newsItems.description: The JD(U)-BJP alliance wins a historic landslide of 206 out of 243 seats, reducing the RJD to 22. Nitish Kumar is sworn in as CM.

reactionOptions.text: Declare this as the triumph of development, and announce new road projects.

risk.badOutcome: Opposition alleges EVM tampering in three urban districts.

reactionOptions.text: Accept the mandate, stating that RJD will reorganize its cadres to defend secularism.

risk.badOutcome: Local party workers express frustration over losing cabinet influence.

reactionOptions.text: Propose forming a bipartisan state development board to recommend highway priorities.

risk.badOutcome: Fierce leader rivalries prevent the board from holding its first meeting.

reactionOptions.text: No comment.

newsItems.title: New Cabinet Approves Hikes in Widows and Disability Pensions
newsItems.description: The newly sworn-in cabinet approves doubling the monthly welfare pension for widows and disabled, as a post-election developmental action.

reactionOptions.text: Rollout the pension hikes immediately through block welfare officers.

risk.badOutcome: Audit reports expose siphoning of pension funds in two districts, drawing bad press.

reactionOptions.text: Demand the government further increase the pension to match inflation rates, calling the hike small.

risk.badOutcome: Finance department states budget limits prevent further hikes.

reactionOptions.text: Propose setting up block-level audit boards with parent and youth representatives.

risk.badOutcome: Lack of volunteer interest keeps the audit boards vacant.

reactionOptions.text: No comment.
